type: Solution Concept Model
title: "Compassionate Intervention Solution Concept Model"
description: "Alberta's Compassionate Intervention Act (Bill 53), which received royal assent on May 15, 2025, establishes a legal framework to support the stabilization, assessment, and treatment of individuals..."
timestamp: 2026-06-26T19:19:07Z
---

## 1. Executive Summary

Alberta's Compassionate Intervention Act (Bill 53), which received royal assent on May 15, 2025, establishes a legal framework to support the stabilization, assessment, and treatment of individuals whose severe substance use or addiction is likely to cause substantial harm to themselves or others. This recovery-oriented approach represents a life-saving intervention to support Albertans experiencing severe addiction.

This is a solution cone provides architectural narrative for the Compassionate Intervention Release of Information (CI ROI) solution.  This solution is intended to support the first step in the Compassionate Intervention workflow where the request for intervention has been received and needs to be assessed by 

The architecture integrates with Epic Connect Care (Alberta's provincial clinical information system), Alberta Netcare (the provincial Electronic Health Record), Recovery Alberta's operational systems, and various data warehousing platforms to create a comprehensive health information summary supporting treatment decisions and continuity of care.

---

## 2. Program Context

### 2.1 Alberta Compassionate Intervention Program

The Compassionate Intervention Act, which received royal assent on May 15, 2025, establishes a legal framework to support the stabilization, assessment, and treatment of individuals whose severe substance use or addiction is likely to cause substantial harm to themselves or others. This recovery-oriented approach represents a life-saving intervention to support Albertans experiencing severe addiction.

**Key Program Elements:**

- Adult family members, guardians, healthcare professionals, police, or peace officers may request assessment orders
- Independent Compassionate Intervention Commission makes legally binding treatment decisions
- Secure care plans provide up to 3 months of treatment in dedicated intervention centres
- Community-based care plans provide up to 6 months of treatment in community settings
- $180 million invested to build two 150-bed Compassionate Intervention Centres in Edmonton and Calgary
- Construction expected to begin in 2026 with completion targeted for 2029

### 2.2 Recovery Alberta

Recovery Alberta is the province's lead provider of mental health, addiction, and correctional health services. Established as part of Alberta's healthcare refocusing initiative, Recovery Alberta operates with approximately 10,000 staff and physicians who transferred from Alberta Health Services. The organization delivers publicly-funded services across acute care, outpatient, virtual, detoxification, and standalone psychiatric settings, along with community outreach and bed-based treatment programs.

Recovery Alberta will operate the Compassionate Intervention Centres and deliver care under the Act, requiring comprehensive access to patient health information from multiple provincial systems to support treatment planning and care coordination.

### 2.3 Canadian Centre of Recovery Excellence (CoRE)

CoRE is a provincial Crown corporation established to ensure Alberta leads in developing data-driven policies and best practices for mental health and addiction recovery. CoRE published a policy white paper on effective compassionate intervention that provides evidence-based recommendations being incorporated into Alberta's framework, including guidance on treatment duration, evidence-based medications, ongoing monitoring, and structured aftercare.

---

## 3. Solution Architecture Overview

![](data:image/png;base64,)

### 3.1 Architecture Purpose

The CI ROI Solution Architecture establishes the data flows and system integrations required to aggregate comprehensive patient health information from disparate provincial systems into a unified health care summary. This summary supports clinical decision-making for individuals subject to compassionate intervention orders, enabling treatment teams to deliver evidence-based, trauma-informed care.

### 3.2 Key Architectural Principles

|Principle|Description|
|---|---|
|**Patient-Centred Integration**|Aggregate health information from the patient's perspective across all care settings|
|**Privacy by Design**|Comply with Alberta's Health Information Act (HIA) and ensure information is accessed only on a need-to-know basis|
|**Statutory Authority-Driven Access**|Leverage Compassionate Intervention Act statutory authority for data release; release records created via Epic ROI Web Service API for audit trail. FHIR Consent is a future enhancement.|
|**Interoperability**|Utilize HL7 FHIR standards for healthcare data exchange where applicable|
|**Audit Trail**|Maintain comprehensive logging of all data access and disclosure activities|
|**Minimal Data Collection**|Collect and disclose only the information necessary to support treatment planning|

---

## 4. System Component Narratives

### 4.1 Compassionate Intervention Statutory Director

|Attribute|Value|
|---|---|
|**Component Type**|Application / Business Actor|
|**Owner**|Recovery Alberta|

The Statutory Director serves as the administrative authority responsible for receiving and processing applications for compassionate intervention. Under the Act, applications are reviewed for completeness by the Statutory Director before being forwarded to a lawyer member of the Compassionate Intervention Commission for determination on apprehension.

**Key Functions:**

- Receive and validate compassionate intervention applications from eligible applicants (family members, guardians, healthcare professionals, police/peace officers)
- Initiate Release of Information requests for relevant health records
- Coordinate with the Compassionate Intervention Commission for legally binding decisions
- Maintain case management workflow throughout the intervention process
- Ensure procedural fairness and protection of individual rights

---

### 4.2 Recovery Alberta Compassionate Intervention Application (RACIA)

|Attribute|Value|
|---|---|
|**Component Type**|Application System|
|**Owner**|Recovery Alberta|

RACIA is the purpose-built application supporting the compassionate intervention workflow. It serves as the system of record for intervention cases and manages the complex data flows required to assemble comprehensive health information summaries.

**Integration Interfaces:**

|Interface|Direction|Description|
|---|---|---|
|**RACIA Outbound ROI Request**|Outbound|Initiates ROI requests to HSS Integration Services, which creates a release record in Epic via the `CreateRelease2` SOAP method (ROI Web Service API, spec #5450). Patient identity (PHN → Epic Patient ID) is resolved in advance via Epic's Patient Lookup Web Service (spec #5454).|
|**RACIA Inbound Automated Encounter Summary**|Inbound|Receives JSON encounter summary payload from HSS (automated path) — aggregated cross-CIS data from Snowflake, triggered by successful `CreateRelease2` response|
|**RACIA Inbound Clinician Response Document**|Inbound|Receives clinician-prepared response document from HSS (clinician-prepared path) — document attached by clinician in Epic HIM and pushed outbound via Epic Bridges|
|**RACIA Inbound CI ROI Report (.pdf)**|Inbound|Receives finalized PDF health care summaries for case documentation|

---

### 4.3 Epic (Provincial Connect Care)

|Attribute|Value|
|---|---|
|**Component Type**|Clinical Information System|
|**Owner**|Alberta Health Services|
|**Platform**|Epic Systems Corporation|

Connect Care is Alberta Health Services' comprehensive clinical information system built on the Epic platform. Implemented through a $1.6 billion initiative beginning in 2016, it serves as the primary electronic health record for AHS facilities, replacing over 1,300 legacy health information systems. Connect Care implements a "one patient, one record" model, providing a consolidated view of patient health information across hospitals, ambulatory clinics, and continuing care settings within AHS.

For the CI ROI solution, Connect Care serves as the authoritative source for acute care encounters, emergency department visits, hospital admissions, clinical documentation, and care provider orders.

#### 4.3.1 EpicCare Link (Provider Portal)

|Attribute|Value|
|---|---|
|**Component Type**|Web Portal|
|**Parent System**|Epic Connect Care|

Web-based portal enabling external providers to access Connect Care patient information, request consults, schedule appointments, place orders, and exchange secure messages. For compassionate intervention, EpicCare Link hosts:

- **Compassionate Intervention Release of Information Request**: Workflow for initiating and tracking ROI requests
- **Compassionate Intervention Chart View**: Read-only access to patient clinical information for authorized CI treatment team members

#### 4.3.2 Epic - Health Information Management (HIM)

|Attribute|Value|
|---|---|
|**Component Type**|Epic Module|
|**Parent System**|Epic Connect Care|

Epic module managing Release of Information functions. For the automated path, HIM receives the release record created via `CreateRelease2` as an audit trail — no clinician approval gate is required under statutory authority. For the clinician-prepared path, HIM staff review the request, prepare and attach a scoped response document, which is then pushed outbound via Epic Bridges.

#### 4.3.3 Epic - Release of Information

|Attribute|Value|
|---|---|
|**Component Type**|Epic Module|
|**Parent System**|Epic Connect Care|

Core Epic functionality for managing formal requests for patient health information. Integrates with HIM workflows to ensure proper authorization, logging, and audit trail for all disclosures.

#### 4.3.4 Epic Bridges

|Attribute|Value|
|---|---|
|**Component Type**|Integration Engine|
|**Parent System**|Epic Connect Care|

Integration engine facilitating data exchange between Epic and external systems. For CI ROI:

- Pushes clinician-prepared response documents outbound to HSS when a clinician completes a release in HIM (clinician-prepared path)
- Manages HL7 messaging with provincial integration platforms
- Routes outbound messages to the IBM Integration Engine via configured endpoints

#### 4.3.5 Connect Care Release of Information Interface

|Attribute|Value|
|---|---|
|**Component Type**|Integration Interface|
|**Parent System**|Epic Connect Care|

Technical interface that supports Release of Information data extraction and delivery. For the CI ROI solution:

- Release records are created in Epic via the `CreateRelease2` SOAP method (ROI Web Service API, spec #5450) called by HSS through the Akana API Gateway
- For the clinician-prepared path, this interface formats the clinician's attached response document and routes it outbound via Epic Bridges to the IBM Integration Engine
- Release records are updated via the `UpdateRelease2` SOAP method after automated delivery (status set to Complete, FulfilledDate recorded)

#### 4.3.6 Epic - Chronicles

|Attribute|Value|
|---|---|
|**Component Type**|Operational Database|
|**Parent System**|Epic Connect Care|

Epic's operational database serving as the real-time source of truth for patient encounters. Chronicles uses a proprietary non-SQL data structure optimized for clinical workflows. Data feeds downstream to the Clarity replica through daily Extract and Load processes.

#### 4.3.7 Epic - ASAP (Emergency)

|Attribute|Value|
|---|---|
|**Component Type**|Epic Module|
|**Parent System**|Epic Connect Care|

Epic's emergency department module tracking patient arrivals, triage assessments, treatments, and dispositions. Critical for compassionate intervention as it captures:

- Emergency presentations related to substance use
- Overdose events and interventions
- Acute intoxication episodes
- Substance-related injuries and medical emergencies

---

### 4.4 FHIR Consent Resource

|Attribute|Value|
|---|---|
|**Component Type**|Data Object / HL7 FHIR Standard|
|**Standard**|HL7 FHIR R4 Consent Resource|

The HL7 FHIR Consent resource provides a standardized mechanism for expressing privacy consent directives in a machine-readable format. In the CI ROI architecture, the FHIR Consent resource may be used as a **future enhancement** to capture the authorization for health information release under the Compassionate Intervention Act in a machine-readable, interoperable format.

**Current Implementation:** The CI ROI solution uses Epic's proprietary ROI Web Service API (`CreateRelease2` / `UpdateRelease2` SOAP methods, spec #5450) to create and manage release records. The statutory authority reference is captured in the `Purpose` and `Comments` fields of the `CreateRelease2` request. This approach was selected because the ROI Web Service is Epic's supported API for programmatic ROI integration — Epic does not expose HIM ROI functionality through FHIR interfaces.

**Future State — Consent Resource Elements for CI ROI:**

|Element|CI ROI Usage|
|---|---|
|**status**|Active consent for current intervention period|
|**scope**|Privacy consent directive for data disclosure|
|**category**|Release of Information under Compassionate Intervention Act authority|
|**patient**|Reference to individual subject to intervention order|
|**performer**|Statutory Director or authorized requestor|
|**organization**|Recovery Alberta / Compassionate Intervention Commission|
|**provision.type**|Permit (authorization to release specified information)|
|**provision.period**|Time-bound validity aligned with care plan duration|
|**provision.actor**|Authorized recipients (CI treatment team members)|
|**provision.action**|Access (read-only disclosure)|
|**provision.class**|Resource types authorized for release|

A future migration to FHIR Consent would enable:

- Machine-readable consent that can be automatically validated
- Audit trail of consent-based data access
- Alignment with emerging interoperability standards
- Future integration with provincial consent management services

---

### 4.5 Clarity Replica

|Attribute|Value|
|---|---|
|**Component Type**|Data Warehouse / Reporting Database|
|**Platform**|Microsoft SQL Server|
|**Parent System**|Epic Connect Care|

Epic Clarity is a reporting database that receives daily extracts from Chronicles, providing a SQL-accessible relational data structure for analytics and reporting without impacting the operational system. Clarity uses an Extract-Transform-Load (ETL) process that runs nightly (midnight to 7:30 AM), meaning the data represents a snapshot from the previous 24-hour period.

**Data Entities for CI ROI:**

|Entity|Description|
|---|---|
|**Hospital Encounters**|Inpatient admissions, observation stays, and procedural encounters|
|**ED Visits**|Emergency department encounters including triage, treatment, and disposition|

These entities are aggregated into the Compassionate Intervention Release of Information Data Product in Snowflake. For compassionate intervention purposes, the nightly latency is acceptable for historical record compilation while near-real-time data needs are served through direct Epic access.

---

### 4.6 HSS Integration Services

|Attribute|Value|
|---|---|
|**Component Type**|Integration Platform|
|**Technologies**|IBM Integration Engine, Akana API Gateway|

HSS Integration Services serve as the middleware layer enabling data exchange between the CMI Application, Epic Connect Care, and the Snowflake data platform. The IBM Integration Engine handles message transformation, routing, and orchestration; the Akana API Gateway handles authentication, rate limiting, and API governance for all calls to Epic.

**Key Functions:**

- Receive ROI requests from the CMI Application
- Resolve patient identity (PHN → Epic Patient ID) via Epic's Patient Lookup Web Service (spec #5454)
- Create release records in Epic HIM via `CreateRelease2` SOAP method (ROI Web Service API, spec #5450)
- **Automated path:** On successful `CreateRelease2` response, query Snowflake for cross-CIS encounter summary and deliver to CMI via webhook
- **Clinician-prepared path:** Receive clinician response documents pushed outbound by Epic Bridges and deliver to CMI via webhook
- Update release records in Epic via `UpdateRelease2` SOAP method (mark Complete after automated delivery)
- Log all disclosures for HIA audit compliance

**Output Interfaces:**

|Interface|Destination|Format|
|---|---|---|
|Automated Encounter Summary Webhook|CMI Application|JSON|
|Clinician Response Document Webhook|CMI Application|JSON (document payload)|
|Compassionate Intervention Release of Information Summary Report|RACIA|PDF|

---

### 4.7 ePCR Siren

|Attribute|Value|
|---|---|
|**Component Type**|Clinical System / Data Source|
|**Vendor**|Medusa Medical Technologies (ESO)|
|**Owner**|Alberta Health Services EMS|

Siren ePCR Suite is the electronic Patient Care Record system used by Alberta's Emergency Medical Services. The provincial ePCR implementation was the first clinical system implemented provincially by AHS. The system captures prehospital care documentation including:

- Patient assessments and chief complaints
- Vital signs and clinical observations
- Treatments and medications administered
- Transport information and receiving facility
- Overdose events and naloxone administration
- Substance-related emergency responses

**Data Architecture:**

```
ePCR Siren (Field User)
    │
    ▼
ePCR Operational Data Store (Data Warehouse)
    │
    ├──► ePCR Operational Data Store Replica (Fall 2025 forward)
    │         └──► ePCR Patient Care Record
    │
    └──► ePCR Operational Data Store Replica (Fall 2025 and earlier)
              └──► ePCR Patient Care Record
```

Both replicas feed into the Compassionate Intervention ROI Data Product through Extract and Load processes.

---

### 4.8 Quanum

|Attribute|Value|
|---|---|
|**Component Type**|Clinical System / Data Source|
|**Data Type**|Patient Care Record (PCR)|

Quanum provides Patient Care Record data to the solution architecture. This system contributes additional clinical documentation to support comprehensive health information assembly for compassionate intervention cases. Data flows from Quanum to the ePCR Siren ecosystem for consolidation with emergency medical services records.

---

### 4.9 Alberta Netcare

|Attribute|Value|
|---|---|
|**Component Type**|Provincial Electronic Health Record|
|**Owner**|Alberta Health (Primary and Preventative Health Services)|

Alberta Netcare is the provincial Electronic Health Record (EHR) system, serving as a secure and confidential repository of health information collected from point-of-care systems across the province. Netcare aggregates data from over 200 sources spanning all sectors of the health continuum and is used by over 50,000 care providers and health administrators.

**Data Available in Netcare:**

|Category|Content|
|---|---|
|**Demographics**|Patient identification and contact information|
|**Event History**|Healthcare encounters across settings|
|**Laboratory Results**|Provincial lab test results|
|**Diagnostic Imaging**|Reports and images|
|**Immunizations**|Vaccination records|
|**Medications**|Dispensed medications from PIN|
|**Transcribed Reports**|Clinical reports and summaries|
|**Community Reports**|Primary care encounter information|

For compassionate intervention, Netcare provides the **EMS Event Summary** data that complements the detailed ePCR records, ensuring access to the patient's longitudinal health record across community and acute care settings.

---

### 4.10 Pharmacy Information Network (PIN)

|Attribute|Value|
|---|---|
|**Component Type**|Provincial Drug Information Repository|
|**Owner**|Alberta Health|
|**Integration**|Real-time HL7 messaging|

The Pharmacy Information Network serves as Alberta's drug information repository, the first application created within the provincial EHR. PIN collects medication dispense data from community and outpatient pharmacies across Alberta and creates a medication profile for each patient.

**PIN Data Elements:**

|Element|Description|
|---|---|
|**Prescriptions**|Prescribed medications with dosage and instructions|
|**Dispenses**|Actual medications dispensed with dates and quantities|
|**Allergies/Intolerances**|Patient-reported and clinician-documented reactions|
|**Drug Interactions**|Alerts from First Data Bank drug database|
|**Tracked Prescriptions**|Monitored substances under TPP Alberta|

For compassionate intervention cases, PIN data is critical for:

- Understanding medication history and adherence patterns
- Identifying potentially problematic prescribing (e.g., multiple prescribers)
- Supporting medication-assisted treatment planning (e.g., opioid agonist therapy)
- Reconciling current medications during stabilization

Dispensed medications data flows through PIN to the broader Netcare ecosystem, accessible via Alberta Netcare Portal. Note: Hospital pharmacy dispenses are not currently submitted to PIN.

---

### 4.11 Snowflake Cloud Data Warehouse PRD

|Attribute|Value|
|---|---|
|**Component Type**|Enterprise Data Warehouse|
|**Platform**|Snowflake AI Data Cloud|
|**Environment**|Production (PRD)|

The Snowflake Cloud Data Warehouse serves as the central analytics platform where the **Compassionate Intervention Release of Information Data Product** is assembled.

**Data Product Aggregation:**

|Source|Data Entity|Aggregation Method|
|---|---|---|
|Clarity Replica|Hospital Encounters|Aggregated in|
|Clarity Replica|ED Visits|Aggregated in|
|ePCR Operational Data Store Replica (Fall 2025 forward)|ePCR Patient Care Record|Aggregated in|
|ePCR Operational Data Store Replica (Fall 2025 and earlier)|ePCR Patient Care Record|Aggregated in|

**Snowflake Capabilities for CI ROI:**

- Secure data sharing with governed access controls
- Role-based access aligned with HIA requirements
- Advanced analytics for pattern identification
- Scalable compute for large-scale data processing
- Integration with Tableau for visualization

The CI ROI Data Product provides the foundation for generating the Compassionate Intervention Release of Information Health Care Summary.

---

### 4.12 Tableau Server

|Attribute|Value|
|---|---|
|**Component Type**|Business Intelligence / Visualization Platform|
|**Deployment**|Enterprise Server|

Tableau Server hosts the **Compassionate Intervention Release of Information Health Care Summary** dashboard. This visualization layer transforms the aggregated CI ROI Data Product from Snowflake into actionable clinical intelligence.

**Dashboard Capabilities:**

|Feature|Description|
|---|---|
|**Encounter Timeline**|Chronological view of healthcare encounters|
|**ED Utilization Patterns**|Emergency department visit frequency and reasons|
|**EMS Response History**|Prehospital care events including overdose responses|
|**Medication Profile**|Dispensed medications and prescribing patterns|
|**Care Provider Network**|Healthcare providers involved in patient care|

The Compassionate Intervention Release of Information Summary Report accesses this dashboard, enabling treatment teams to quickly understand a patient's health trajectory and inform individualized care planning.

---

### 4.13 Health Analytics Portal (HAP)

|Attribute|Value|
|---|---|
|**Component Type**|Analytics Platform / Portal|
|**Owner**|Alberta Health Services|
|**Classification**|Confidential|

The Health Analytics Portal provides access to trusted secondary use data, information products, and supporting services for authorized analysts and researchers. HAP reflects Alberta's commitment to leveraging research and innovation to improve healthcare.

For compassionate intervention, HAP hosts confidential analytical content supporting:

- Program evaluation and outcomes measurement
- Population health monitoring for addiction trends
- Research on intervention effectiveness
- Policy development support

Access is governed by data sharing agreements and privacy requirements under the Health Information Act.

---

## 5. Data Flow Summary

### 5.1 Release of Information Request Flow

```
┌─────────────────────────────┐
│ Statutory Director          │
│ (Initiates ROI Request)     │
└─────────────┬───────────────┘
              │
              ▼
┌─────────────────────────────┐
│ RACIA / CMI Application     │
│ (POST /roi-request to HSS)  │
└─────────────┬───────────────┘
              │
              ▼
┌─────────────────────────────┐
│ HSS Integration Services    │
│ (IBM Integration Engine +   │
│  Akana API Gateway)         │
└─────────────┬───────────────┘
              │ CreateRelease2 SOAP
              ▼
┌─────────────────────────────┐
│ Epic ROI Web Service API    │
│ (Creates release record     │
│  in HIM — spec #5450)       │
└─────────────┬───────────────┘
              │
       ┌──────┴──────┐
       ▼             ▼
┌────────────┐ ┌────────────────┐
│ Automated  │ │ Clinician-     │
│ Path:      │ │ Prepared Path: │
│ Snowflake  │ │ Clinician      │
│ query →    │ │ attaches doc → │
│ webhook    │ │ Epic Bridges → │
│ to CMI     │ │ HSS → webhook  │
└────────────┘ │ to CMI         │
               └────────────────┘
```

### 5.2 Data Aggregation Flow

|Source System|Data Entity|Process|Destination|
|---|---|---|---|
|Epic Chronicles|Encounter Data|Daily Extract and Load|Clarity Replica|
|Clarity Replica|Hospital Encounters|Aggregated in|Snowflake CI ROI Data Product|
|Clarity Replica|ED Visits|Aggregated in|Snowflake CI ROI Data Product|
|ePCR Siren|Patient Care Records|Extract and Load|ePCR Operational Data Store|
|ePCR Data Stores|ePCR Patient Care Record|Aggregated in|Snowflake CI ROI Data Product|
|Quanum|Patient Care Records|Flows to|ePCR Siren ecosystem|
|Alberta Netcare|EMS Event Summary|Flows to|ePCR Siren ecosystem|
|Snowflake|CI ROI Data Product|Accesses|Tableau Server|

### 5.3 Response Flow to RACIA / CMI Application

|Response Type|Source|Trigger|Interface|Format|
|---|---|---|---|---|
|Automated Encounter Summary|HSS Integration Services → Snowflake|Successful `CreateRelease2` response (ReleaseID returned)|Webhook to CMI Application|JSON|
|Clinician-Prepared Document|Epic HIM → Epic Bridges → HSS|Clinician completes review and attaches document in HIM; Epic Bridges pushes outbound|Webhook to CMI Application|JSON (document payload)|
|Health Care Summary Report|Tableau / Snowflake|CI ROI Data Product query|RACIA Inbound CI ROI Report|PDF|

---

## 6. Governance and Compliance

### 6.1 Health Information Act Compliance

All data collection, use, and disclosure within the CI ROI solution architecture complies with Alberta's Health Information Act (HIA). Key compliance requirements:

|Requirement|Implementation|
|---|---|
|**Authorized Access**|Role-based access controls limiting data access to authorized CI treatment team members|
|**Need-to-Know**|Data disclosure limited to information necessary for treatment planning|
|**Audit Logging**|Comprehensive logging of all data access and disclosure activities|
|**Privacy Impact Assessment**|PIA completed for CI ROI solution prior to implementation|
|**Data Sharing Agreements**|Formal agreements between custodians governing information exchange|

### 6.2 Compassionate Intervention Act Safeguards

The Compassionate Intervention Act includes built-in safeguards to ensure individual rights are protected:

- **Right to Legal Counsel**: Individuals informed of right to legal representation
- **Mental Health Patient Advocate**: Access to Alberta Mental Health Patient Advocate
- **Appeal Rights**: Individuals and guardians may appeal care plan decisions
- **Independent Commission**: Legally binding decisions made by independent body, not treatment providers
- **Privacy Law Compliance**: Collection, use, and disclosure compliant with HIA, PIPA, and FOIP
- **Treatment Consent**: Individuals/guardians must agree to most treatment (limited exceptions specified in legislation)

### 6.3 Data Custodianship

|Custodian|Data Holdings|Responsibility|
|---|---|---|
|Alberta Health Services|Connect Care, Clarity, ePCR|Clinical encounter data custody|
|Alberta Health|Netcare, PIN|Provincial EHR and drug information custody|
|Recovery Alberta|RACIA, CI case records|Compassionate intervention case custody|

The CI ROI architecture respects custodianship boundaries while enabling authorized information sharing for compassionate intervention purposes through formal consent mechanisms.

### 6.4 Statutory Immunity

The Compassionate Intervention Act provides statutory immunity from liability for those acting in good faith in the detention, assessment, and treatment of individuals under the Act. This immunity does not extend to professional regulatory proceedings for unprofessional conduct.

---

## 7. Conclusion

The Compassionate Intervention Release of Information Solution Architecture provides a comprehensive framework for assembling patient health information to support treatment decisions under Alberta's first-in-Canada Compassionate Intervention Act. By integrating data from Epic Connect Care, Alberta Netcare, Emergency Medical Services, and pharmacy systems through Snowflake analytics and Tableau visualization, the solution enables Recovery Alberta clinicians to deliver evidence-based, trauma-informed care to individuals experiencing severe substance use disorders.

The architecture balances the imperative for comprehensive health information access with robust privacy protections, consent management, and governance controls. As the Compassionate Intervention program moves toward full implementation with centre construction beginning in 2026, this solution architecture will continue to evolve to meet operational requirements while maintaining compliance with Alberta's health information legislation.

---

## Appendix A: Acronyms and Abbreviations

|Acronym|Definition|
|---|---|
|AHS|Alberta Health Services|
|CCD|Continuity of Care Document|
|CI|Compassionate Intervention|
|CIS|Clinical Information System|
|CoRE|Canadian Centre of Recovery Excellence|
|ED|Emergency Department|
|EHR|Electronic Health Record|
|EMS|Emergency Medical Services|
|ePCR|Electronic Patient Care Record|
|ETL|Extract-Transform-Load|
|FHIR|Fast Healthcare Interoperability Resources|
|HAP|Health Analytics Portal|
|HIA|Health Information Act|
|HIM|Health Information Management|
|HL7|Health Level Seven International|
|JCAPS|Java Composite Application Platform Suite (legacy — current solution uses IBM Integration Engine)|
|PCR|Patient Care Record|
|pHIE|Provincial Health Information Exchange (legacy — current solution routes through Akana API Gateway)|
|PIN|Pharmaceutical Information Network|
|RACIA|Recovery Alberta Compassionate Intervention Application|
|ROI|Release of Information|
|TPP|Tracked Prescription Program|

---
